You survived with the help of your hardlight tent, leaving you free to override the automatic lockdown and travel at your leisure. At least you won’t have to worry about catching a cold in here!

original: Your musings are cut short by an abruptly blaring alarm. “Tank pressure rising to unsafe levels!” The muted amber lighting gives way to flashing red. “Seal failure in tank 2!” Behind you, you see a tank with the bold ‘2’ stamped on its surface spraying caustic juice across the door. “Rupture! Evacuate! Sealing chamber. Total containment failure imminent!” The words fly out faster than you can react. The southern door audibly clicks as it seals, locking you inside.
translation: 你的沉思被突然响起的警报打断。“油箱压力升至不安全水平！” 原本柔和的琥珀色灯光转为闪烁的红色。“2号油箱密封失效！” 你身后，一个印着醒目‘2’字样的油箱正朝门上喷洒腐蚀性液体。“破裂！紧急疏散！密封室封闭中。全面失效难以避免！” 一连串信息扑面而来，快得你来不及反应。南边的门发出清晰的咔嗒声，锁死了，把你困在里面。

original: You deploy the tent and climb inside as you hear another tank rupture. The next blows, and you seal yourself inside, listening to the one after that shatter. It’s like listening to a chaingun in a typhoon: constant cracks and booms blending together with a near-constant pitter-patter. Since the tent itself is formed of hardlight, you can use the controls to turn sections transparent - and watch the entire system finish failing, splashing disinfectant onto the crackling, projected barrier.
translation: 你撑开帐篷钻了进去，又一声罐体炸裂传来。紧接着又是一声爆响，你封好帐篷，听着下一个罐子碎裂。那动静，活像台风里夹着连发机炮——噼啪的爆裂声、轰隆的炸响，混着几乎没停过的淅沥声，搅成一团。帐篷本身是硬光造的，你调了调控制，让几块区域变透明——眼看着整个系统彻底崩溃，消毒液溅上噼啪作响的投射屏障。
context: <<POS:00284199>> output("You deploy the tent and climb inside as you hear another tank rupture. The next blows, and you seal yourself inside, listening to the one after that shatter. You could even dial the Gs up or down, if you felt like deviating from the Terran standard employed on most Steele Tech-operated stations.\n\nThe western door is still broken, of course, but you can squeeze through the gap in the frame, even if gravity makes for some uncomfortable wiggling.

original: Standing before you is a seven foot tall mountain of dense muscle and orange-skinned might. The tiger-striped kaithrit covers most of his colorful patterns in the armored sleeve of a Steele Tech hazard suit, but there’s more than enough of his tails, claws, and fluffy ears showing for you to know exactly how much he resembles the ancient big cats found on old Terra. His gaze is steady from behind the AR visor shrouding one slitted eye, and his grip on his weapons is firm. An ancient but serviceable pistol threatens to bark out a barrage of deadly, heavy leaden rounds while the sledge held securely in his off-hand crackles with surges of vision-searing electricity.

original: “How’s my health looking?” you ask her. “You do not appear to have any infections!” the medical robot replies helpfully. “Which is more than I can say for several of my most recent patients. My, my, it seems that after several years without any medical incidents whatsoever on the station, I was reactivated just in time to treat an outbreak of sexually-transmitted mutagenic diseases!” You blink. “W-what.” Tika nods excitedly. “The galaxy is full of many terrible illnesses, but most life-threatening ones can be easily defeated by microsurgeons, such as the ones I detect in your own bloodstream! However, there are strains of mutagenic, transformative diseases which are sexually transmitted — usually between un-uplifted species and the galaxy’s poor — which microsurgeons cannot defeat on their own. These diseases are technically harmless, but do cause physical appearance changes, such as hair growth across the body, enlarged sexual attributes, or changes to coloration. Even worse, my psychological profile indicates that some individuals may intentionally infect themselves with transformational illnesses as a cost-saving alternative to targeted body modification. Imagine infecting yourself with a disease for cosmetic affect!” The robot shakes her head in an affectation of exasperation. “Luckily, you are clear of any such infection. Steele Industrial Technology and Production always urges its employees to practice safe sex and maintain a clean bill of health. My behavioral protocols indicate I should advise you to do the same.” You’ll have to keep that in mind. Maybe she could give you something to keep that from happening...?

My role here is to provide supplementary medical care to the workers on the mining platform, but please note that I am not a replacement for an emergency medical crew. I cannot perform complex surgery or respond to calls outside the reach of my mounting. As a prototype myself, I was also deemed a valid platform to test experimental treatments on consenting workers. Two birds with one stone, as the saying goes.” Well, Tika being a prototype explains why you’ve never seen a robot quite like her before. You guess Steele Tech didn’t bother going into production on a whole line of Tikas. Not too surprising considering she can’t even move. Still... Victor Steele does not like to waste perfectly good resources, especially a perfectly functional T1-K4 prototype! A company representative will come any day now to retrieve me!” You glance over your shoulder at the dusty, dark hallway you just came out of. [pc.isMischievous |“Yeah, sure... they’ll be back any time now. Riiiight.” “Your sudden change in pitch indicates you are lying. But why...” |“I’m not sure they’re coming back, Tika...” “Surely you are mistaken!” ] the robotic nurse huffs, frowning. “My overseer assured me...” Seeing how long this place has been abandoned by anybody but outlaw scavengers, it’s hard to believe there’s any hope of anyone from Steele Tech coming back here. Though that does make your situation feel more dire, too. There’s certainly no rescue party coming. Tika forces a little laugh. “Well, until my overseer returns, please feel free to make use of any of my services. It is important to be useful, after all, and I am confident that the research and development group will be most pleased with all the diagnostics I have collected since the last time they checked up on me. Perhaps they are simply waiting until I hit a data-gathering milestone...” [pc.isAss |Damn, this robot’s really acting like it’s a person. Like she’s lonely. What kinda jackass programs a robot to sound like that, huh? |Aw. She may just be a computer, but even still... that’s just kinda sad. ]

original: [flags.MET_TIKA |The mining facility’s medical office is a small affair, dominated by T1-K4’s dock on the far wall and a couple of canvas cots for her patients to lie on, close enough to be in easy reach of the stationary medical ‘bot. The office has been kept clean, at least, though its supply cabinets have been thoroughly ransacked, with broken glass and discarded wrappings overflowing from the (likely blocked-off) garbage chute. |[var0 |Hoping to find something to treat your injuries |Curious as to what you might find in an abandoned medbay ], you test the door — and find it unlocked. It creaks open noisily, releasing a hiss of stale air as you step inside. There’s only red emergency lighting inside, but they’re enough to make out a couple of canvas cots in the middle of the office, a counter with the remains of a holo-projector hanging off the edge by its own naked wires, and what at first looks like the bottom half of a vending machine. And there’s a girl sitting on top of it, dressed all in shiny black, with a bob of golden hair sitting under a white beret with a red caduceus. Except, she’s not sitting on the machine, as your eyes wander down, over slender curves and an outfit that’s too tight to her frame even for latex. From the hip down, her body melds into the machine, replacing her legs with an expanse of plastic and steel. A robot! It’s still active, too, watching your reactions with faintly-glowing aqua eyes... or maybe scanning you, steadily looking over every inch of you before she (it?) speaks. [var1 |“[var0 |[var2 |Oh dear, oh no, you’re in rough shape! Injures and an infection! |Oh no! You’ve been injured, how horrible! ] |Oh no, you’re suffering from an infection! ] Please take a seat, you’re first in queue for treatment!” [pc.isMischievous |Yeah, first in line. Guess you beat the traffic. ]You sit yourself down on one of the cots, letting the robotic medic lean over you, looking you over in detail. “I am programmed to provide free medical treatment to employees of Steele Industrial Technology and Production,” she says helpfully, her voice smooth and faintly synthetic. “Unfortunately, you aren’t on the base’s employee register. I’d love to help, but...” She twitches, arms tensing for a moment. “Luckily, my behavioral routines have been damaged or altered, so I’ll do just that. I am T1-K4, or ‘Tika.’ Please just ask me to treat any injuries or infections you need help with and I’ll do my best!” This medical drone doesn’t seem to be exactly in good working order, but then again, you’ll need all the help you can get if you want to make it off this rock. “Please, select any service you require,” she adds, gesturing to a holographic menu that, with some obvious difficulty, flickers to life at her hip. “I also have an inventory of helpful items, which you can exchange galactic standard credits for. Lucky you again — the hotwiring brutes that I have been wasting quality medical care on the last seventy nine hours haven’t tried breaking my secured reserves open to take them.” |“You seem to be in fine shape to work, guest!” the robot says in a smooth synthetic voice, giving you a programmatically puzzled look. “Still, there are no employees of Steele Industrial Technology and Production that currently require my assistance. Please feel free to have a seat and relax. I am T1-K4, or ‘Tika.’ I am not programmed with advanced psychological evaluation protocols, but your vitals indicate that you are under some degree of stress. I would be happy to help in any way I can!” She gestures towards one of the cots in front of her, adding helpfully, “My behavioral protocols have been altered or damaged so that I am compelled to provide free medical care to anyone that asks, so, feel free to come back and ask if you get hurt out there. I’ll do my best to help! Oh, and I also have an inventory of helpful items, which you can exchange galactic standard credits for. Lucky you again — the hotwiring brutes that I have been wasting quality medical care on the last seventy nine hours haven’t tried breaking my secured reserves open to take them.” ] ]

original: Backed up against the hangar’s western wall, you come across a cache of fuel canisters tucked into a nook in the wall of sagging containers to the south. Their lids fled some time ago, and with them, all the valuable fluids within. A damaged pump hose snakes through their midst, discarded by the looters who had their way with this little cache not so long ago. You’ll want to keep your eyes open here...\n\nJust north of here, an emergency airlock offers entrance to the rest of the station. The hangar widens to the east, sprawling expansively as it edges closer to space.
translation: 背靠着机库西墙，你会发现南侧墙边一排摇摇欲坠的集装箱里藏着一堆燃料罐。罐盖早已不见踪影，里面的宝贵液体也随之消失。一根破损的泵管蜿蜒穿过罐子，那是不久前洗劫过这小堆燃料的盗贼丢弃的。你要在这里保持警惕……\n\n这里的北面，有一个紧急气闸通往空间站的其他部分。机库向东延伸，随着它越来越靠近太空，规模也越来越大。

original: While the station’s main lights aren’t on, emergency lights still sputter out enough of a sulfur glow for you to find your way down the lonely passage. The tunnel burrows due west into the station, upwind against an unusually brisk breeze (for the inside of a space station). Opposite lies the entrance to FIRST-14’s hangar, where you parked your ship when you landed. Without the security field to block you, you’re free to return there at any time.
translation: 尽管站内主灯没有开启，但应急灯仍然发出足够的硫磺光，帮助你在这条孤独的通道中找到方向。这条通道向西穿入站内，顶部受到异常清冷的气流影响（对于空间站内部来说）。对面是FIRST-14的机库入口，你停放飞船的地方。没有阻挡你的安全场，你可以随时自由返回那里。

original: Now that FIRST-14’s gravity is turned on, pushing back against the strong headwinds pumping out of the life support system isn’t just possible - it’s relatively easy. The curved surface you walk on does pose its own challenges, but they’re nothing a stabilizing hand braced against the wall can’t fix. Just ahead to the west, you spy an opening in the vent into some kind of maintenance area, judging by the rusty racks on the walls.
translation: 现在，打开FIRST-14的引力系统后，顶住从生命支持系统中喷发出来的强劲逆风不仅变得可能——而且还不太难。你脚下的弧形地面确实会带来一些不便，但只要一只手扶着墙就能轻松解决。往西走，你会看到通风口有个开口，从墙上锈迹斑斑的架子判断，那里似乎是个维修区。

original:
One of the walls bears a faded sign:
Ferro Industrial Reclamation Station T-14 C-Deck Navigational Directory:
  • Communications: Northwest
  • Gravitation: Northeast
  • Hangar: Southeast
  • Medical Bay C: North
  • Custodial / Secondary Life Support Access: Southwest
 This corridor leads north, toward a distant intersection, and south, to an eastward bend.
translation:
其中一堵墙上挂着褪色标志:
费罗工业回收站 T-14 C-甲板 导航目录:
  • 通讯：西北
  • 重力：东北
  • 机库：东南
  • 医疗区 C：北
  • 卫生间/次级生命支持通道：西南
 这条走廊向北通向远处的十字路口，向南则向东拐。

original: FIRST-14’s access corridors are spartan affairs. Industrial and mining stations often are, but seeing one stripped and abandoned is truly next level. Nothing was left in this tunnel, not one single crate, scrap, or battery. The only thing to look at is the rust-spotted deck below and the flickering emergency lights along the walls. A sign hangs further south with what appear to be navigational directions.\n\nTo the north, the tunnel intersects with an east-west passage.
translation: FIRST-14的通道入口是简朴的。工业和采矿站往往如此，但看到一个被剥夺并被遗弃的站点那是另一回事。隧道中没有留下任何东西，连一个箱子、废料或电池都没有。唯一可以看到的就是下面沾满锈迹的甲板和沿墙壁闪烁的应急灯。在更南边悬挂着一个标牌，上面似乎是导航指示。\n\n向北，隧道与东西通道交汇。

original: You stand in the middle of an intersection between two corridors. Both run straight and true, intersecting tangentially with geometric precision. Back south lies the way to the hangar - and your ship. Ahead north, many of the emergency lights have fallen into darkness, making it a path of mystery and danger. Green stripes follow the other tunnel’s east-west route through the station, though the eastern passage bends into a corner near immediately.
translation: 你站在两条走廊的交汇处。两条走廊笔直延伸，以几何般的精确度相切。往南走，通往机库和你的飞船。往北走，许多应急灯都已熄灭，使这条路变得神秘而危险。绿色的条纹沿着另一条隧道的东西走向穿过空间站，但东侧的通道很快就拐进了拐角。

original: Most of the emergency lights in this segment of Ferro Industrial Reclamation Station have fallen dark, shrouding you in permanent night every bit as deep as a subterranean cave’s. Here and there, single survival lamps still glow, sputtering their baleful orange radiance across broken crates and scuffed decks. FIRST-14 grows brighter to the south and dimmer to the north.
translation: 费罗工业回收站这一段区域的大多数应急灯都熄灭了，将你笼罩在和地下洞穴一样深的永久黑夜中。零星散落的求生灯仍在微微发光，昏暗的橙色光芒在破碎的木箱和磨损的甲板上闪烁。FIRST-14 的光线向南逐渐明亮，向北则逐渐昏暗。

original: An open crate rests against the south wall. Without its lid fastened, you’re free to see the pile of copper wire stored inside, apparently ripped haphazardly out of non-essential systems in the wall. Someone took the time to strip the insulating sleeves from the semi-valuable metal before storing it yet didn’t bother to crate up their prize and haul it off.\n\nThe green-striped tunnel veers north and west. Just to the north, you can see a doorway...
translation: 一个敞开的箱子靠在南墙上。箱子盖子没盖好，里面堆放着一堆铜线，显然是从墙里一些无关紧要的管道系统上随意拆下来的。有人费了好大劲儿把这些半值钱的金属上的绝缘层剥掉才把它们放进去，却懒得把这宝贝装箱运走。\n\n这条绿条纹隧道向北偏西延伸。你看到一个门在北边……

original: A doorway in the eastern wall sits half-open, seized in a cock-eyed position incapable of moving without the aid of some heavy equipment. A sign above reads, C-Deck Artificial Gravity Station. You could squeeze in, if you wanted. The pneumatics may be jammed with bent and strained metal, but you can squeeze right through the slanted gap.\n\nThis hallway flexes south and west from here.
translation: 东墙上有一扇半开的门，歪斜地卡住，没有重型机械的帮助根本无法移动。门上方挂着一块牌子，上面写着：C层人工重力站。如果你愿意，或许可以挤进去。气动装置可能被弯曲变形的金属卡住了，但你还是可以挤过这个倾斜的缝隙。\n\n这条走廊从这里向南和向西延伸。

original: A stylized logo of a cross made out of two giant, cartoonish band-aids hangs on the northern wall just above a smaller set of illuminated letters that reads, “The Doctor is IN(stalled)!” It would look a lot less creepy if the backlighting didn’t flicker so crazily. Beneath is a white door, marred by a set of fresh claw marks through the thickly slathered on paint.\n\nThe passageway journeys east and west from here.
translation: 北墙上挂着一个由两个巨大的卡通创可贴组成的十字架标志，标志上方是一组较小的发光字母，上面写着“医生已（安装）到位！”。如果背光不至于闪烁得那么厉害，看起来就不会那么诡异了。标志下方是一扇白门，厚厚的油漆上留有几道新鲜的爪痕。\n\n通道从这里向东西延伸。

original: As the green-lined hallway bends around a 90-degree corner, you spot an elevator entrance splitting the northern wall, almost beckoning you to enter, but the control panel tells a different story. Angry red letters proclaim, “Elevators requisitioned for use by licensed S.C.A.V.S. techs only. Please swipe ID.” It looks like custom software too, not something you’d be able to hack into without more time and patience than you can spare.\n\nIt seems you can’t leave this level of the station, but this hallway journeys east and south.

original: A tiny plastic fan clings on to a ceiling vent with a pink-colored clothespin, resolutely spinning with every breeze the life support system pumps out. Judging by all the dust on it, it’s been here for decades, somehow functional in spite of the ravages of time. In a lot of ways, it’s like the rest of this station. You’ll just have to hope it all holds together long enough for you to make repairs and bail.\n\nThis segment of corridor runs north to a sharp bend at an elevator and south a bit further.
translation: 一个小小的塑料风扇用一个粉色的衣夹夹在天花板的通风口上，随着生命维持系统吹出的每一阵微风，它都坚定地旋转着。从上面的灰尘来看，它已经在这里几十年了，尽管饱经风霜，却依然运转正常。在很多方面，它就像这个空间站的其他地方一样。你只能祈祷它能撑到你完成修理并离开为止。\n\n这段走廊向北延伸到电梯处的急弯，然后向南继续延伸一段距离。

original: Strange, distorted echoes ring through the supposedly abandoned station’s corridors. Hammer blows, small explosions, and the warbling electrochirps of high-end salvage equipment slicing steel like butter combine to tell you one crystal clear fact: FIRST-14 isn’t as empty as it should be. The tunnel bends here, veering north and east through the station’s C-Deck.
translation: 诡异扭曲的回声在看似废弃的车站走廊中回荡。锤击声、小型爆炸声，以及高端打捞设备切割钢铁时发出的嗡嗡作响的电子声，共同揭示了一个清晰的事实：FIRST-14 并不像它应该的那样空无一人。隧道在此弯曲，向北偏东方向延伸，穿过车站的 C 层。
